nodes.name: reconstruction
nodes.description: Ingested pipeline from reconstruction

nodes.name: GAN Patch Reconstruction
nodes.description: Generates a reconstructed signal patch from clean PPG context and injected noise using a provided generator on a specified device.
nodes.type_signature: (ppg_clean: array-like signal segment, noise: array-like latent/noise tensor, sampling_rate: float|int, generator: callable/model, device: string|device handle) -> array-like signal segment
nodes.conceptual_summary: Conditional Latent-to-Segment Generative Synthesis. Maps a conditioning signal segment and a latent random tensor through a learned nonlinear generator to produce a synthetic output segment with matched structural length. The conditioning input constrains coarse morphology, while the latent input injects fine-scale variability drawn from the generator's learned distribution. Execution is an inference-time forward pass on a specified compute target, with no parameter updates in this step.. Applications: Reconstructing short corrupted windows in industrial sensor streams using neighboring clean context plus latent variability., Generating localized texture patches in materials microscopy conditioned on surrounding microstructure tiles., Synthesizing missing waveform fragments in acoustic monitoring conditioned on adjacent clean temporal context., Imputing partial trajectory segments in robotic telemetry conditioned on recent kinematic state history.

nodes.name: initialize_reconstruction_state
nodes.description: Derive fixed reconstruction window settings and initialize iterative state from inputs.

inputs.name: noise
inputs.constraints: target noise sequence; only its length is used

nodes.type_signature: (ppg_clean: np.ndarray, noise: list, sampling_rate: int, shifting_step_seconds: int|float, upsampling_rate: float|int) -> tuple[np.ndarray, int, int, list[float], int, int, int, float|int]

nodes.name: evaluate_reconstruction_completion
nodes.description: Check whether reconstruction should continue based on current and target lengths.
nodes.type_signature: (current_reconstructed_len: int, target_noise_len: int) -> bool

nodes.name: normalize_and_batch_clean_context
nodes.description: Scale clean context and replicate it into a batch tensor for generator inference.
nodes.type_signature: (ppg_clean_current: np.ndarray, batch_size: int) -> tuple[np.ndarray, torch.FloatTensor, int]

nodes.name: generate_noise_candidate_patch
nodes.description: Run generator on prepared batch and extract one reconstructed candidate patch.

outputs.name: noise_candidate
outputs.constraints: 1D generated patch from first batch item

nodes.type_signature: (generator_input: torch.FloatTensor, generator: callable/model, device: str|torch.device) -> np.ndarray

nodes.name: align_and_stitch_signals_by_peak_boundaries
nodes.description: Upsample clean and generated signals, detect peaks, and stitch them at physiologic boundary points.

outputs.name: stitched_signal_upsampled
outputs.constraints: clean prefix + generated suffix in upsampled domain

nodes.type_signature: (ppg_clean_norm: np.ndarray, noise_candidate: np.ndarray, sampling_rate_state: int, upsampling_rate_state: float|int) -> tuple[list[float], int]

nodes.name: resample_stitched_signal_to_native_scale
nodes.description: Project stitched upsampled signal back to native sample count.
nodes.type_signature: (stitched_signal_upsampled: list[float], stitched_native_len: int) -> np.ndarray

nodes.name: update_iteration_state_from_stitched_signal
nodes.description: Extract next reconstructed chunk, update remaining target, append accumulation, and slide clean context window.

outputs.name: ppg_clean_next
outputs.constraints: shifted context window for next iteration

nodes.type_signature: (stitched_signal_native: np.ndarray, clean_len: int, len_rec: int, remaining_noise_current: int, reconstructed_noise_current: list[float]) -> tuple[list[float], int, np.ndarray, int]

nodes.name: Windowed Signal Reconstruction
nodes.description: Constructs a full reconstructed signal from clean/noisy index partitions, with optional filtering controlled by input flag.

inputs.name: sig
inputs.constraints: 1D or channel-first signal supported by implementation

inputs.name: noisy_indices
inputs.constraints: Valid indices into sig; may be disjoint from clean_indices

outputs.name: reconstructed_signal
outputs.constraints: Same length/index domain as input sig

nodes.type_signature: (sig: array-like full signal, clean_indices: index array/list, noisy_indices: index array/list, sampling_rate: float|int, filter_signal: bool|callable) -> array-like full signal
nodes.conceptual_summary: Partitioned Sequence Recomposition with Conditional Denoising. Reassembles an ordered numeric sequence/tensor by writing values back into a shared index domain from multiple index-defined partitions. The method preserves global alignment by enforcing index-consistent placement across all provided subsets. A conditional post-processing branch (boolean- or callable-triggered) optionally applies a smoothing/filtering operator parameterized by a positive sampling-density scalar.. Applications: Reconstructing electrophysiology traces from artifact-marked vs artifact-free sample partitions with optional denoising., Rebuilding seismic waveform streams from trusted and contaminated time windows before frequency-selective cleanup., Composing robotics sensor trajectories from reliable and degraded intervals, then applying optional low-pass stabilization., Reassembling packet-level network telemetry series from validated and suspect segments with optional noise suppression.

nodes.name: load_reconstruction_model_runtime
nodes.description: Prepare GAN runtime artifacts by resolving the trained generator weights and execution device.
nodes.type_signature: (model_path: str, model_file_name: str, generator_class: type, cuda_available: bool) -> tuple[torch.nn.Module, torch.device]

nodes.name: harmonize_sampling_and_partitions
nodes.description: Convert signal and index partitions to the model sampling frequency when needed; otherwise normalize clean indices to a flat representation.

outputs.name: sampling_rate_original
outputs.constraints: Set only when resampling_flag is True

outputs.name: clean_indices_flat
outputs.constraints: Flattened clean indices at working rate

outputs.name: noisy_indices_grouped
outputs.constraints: Grouped noisy indices at working rate

nodes.type_signature: (sig: np.ndarray, clean_indices: list[list[int]], noisy_indices: list[list[int]], sampling_rate: int, model_sampling_rate: int) -> tuple[np.ndarray, int, bool, int|null, float|null, list[int], list[list[int]]]

nodes.name: apply_optional_bandpass_filter
nodes.description: Condition the working-rate signal with a 0.5-3 Hz bandpass when filtering is requested.
nodes.type_signature: (sig_working: np.ndarray, sampling_rate_working: int, filter_signal: bool) -> np.ndarray

nodes.name: derive_scaled_signal_and_window_budget
nodes.description: Standardize the conditioned signal and compute the maximum reconstructable noisy-window length.

outputs.name: max_rec_length
outputs.constraints: Max noisy window size in samples

nodes.type_signature: (sig_conditioned: np.ndarray, sampling_rate_working: int, max_reconstruction_length_sec: float) -> tuple[np.ndarray, int]

nodes.name: identify_eligible_noise_windows
nodes.description: Select noisy segments that are short enough and have a fully clean preceding context window.

inputs.name: noisy_indices_grouped
inputs.constraints: Grouped noisy segments at working rate

outputs.name: candidate_windows
outputs.constraints: Each item includes noise indices and reconstruction context bounds

nodes.type_signature: (noisy_indices_grouped: list[list[int]], clean_indices_flat: list[int], max_rec_length: int) -> list[dict]

nodes.name: generate_noise_reconstructions
nodes.description: Run GAN inference per eligible noisy segment using preceding clean context.

outputs.name: reconstructed_windows
outputs.constraints: Each item adds GAN-reconstructed noisy signal

nodes.type_signature: (candidate_windows: list[dict], sig_conditioned: np.ndarray, sampling_rate_working: int, generator: torch.nn.Module, device: torch.device) -> list[dict]

nodes.name: build_upsampled_alignment_context
nodes.description: Upsample reconstructed and neighboring clean segments and collect peak markers needed for boundary alignment.

outputs.name: alignment_contexts
outputs.constraints: Upsampled segments plus detected peaks and validity metadata

nodes.type_signature: (reconstructed_windows: list[dict], sig_scaled: np.ndarray, sampling_rate_working: int, upsampling_rate: float) -> list[dict]

nodes.name: merge_segments_with_peak_alignment
nodes.description: Construct stitched full-signal candidates by peak-aligned concatenation, skipping contexts that cannot be robustly aligned.

inputs.name: alignment_contexts
inputs.constraints: Upsampled components with optional peak anchors

nodes.type_signature: (alignment_contexts: list[dict], sampling_rate_working: int, upsampling_rate: float) -> list[np.ndarray]

nodes.name: backproject_descale_and_reassess_quality
nodes.description: Project stitched candidates back to working length, descale to physiological range, and recompute clean/noisy partitions via SQA.

outputs.name: reconstruction_states
outputs.constraints: Sequential successful states with ppg_descaled and updated partitions

outputs.name: reconstruction_flag
outputs.constraints: True iff at least one candidate passed

nodes.type_signature: (stitched_signals: list[np.ndarray], sig_scaled: np.ndarray, sig_conditioned: np.ndarray, sampling_rate_working: int) -> tuple[list[dict], bool]

nodes.name: select_effective_signal_and_partitions
nodes.description: Choose the last successful reconstruction state when available; otherwise keep the conditioned signal and current partitions.

inputs.name: reconstruction_flag
inputs.constraints: Indicates whether a valid reconstruction exists

inputs.name: clean_indices_flat
inputs.constraints: Fallback clean indices in flat form

outputs.name: clean_indices_flat_effective
outputs.constraints: Effective clean indices at working rate

outputs.name: noisy_indices_grouped_effective
outputs.constraints: Effective noisy partitions at working rate

nodes.type_signature: (reconstruction_states: list[dict], reconstruction_flag: bool, sig_conditioned: np.ndarray, clean_indices_flat: list[int], noisy_indices_grouped: list[list[int]]) -> tuple[np.ndarray, list[int], list[list[int]]]

nodes.name: finalize_output_rate_and_partition_groups
nodes.description: Return signal and partitions at original sampling rate when needed, and ensure clean/noisy indices are grouped into consecutive segments.

inputs.name: clean_indices_flat_effective
inputs.constraints: Flat clean indices at working rate

inputs.name: resampling_flag
inputs.constraints: Whether to map back to original rate

inputs.name: resampling_rate
inputs.constraints: Index mapping ratio when returning to original rate

outputs.name: ppg_signal
outputs.constraints: Final output signal at original or working rate

nodes.type_signature: (ppg_working: np.ndarray, clean_indices_flat_effective: list[int], noisy_indices_grouped_effective: list[list[int]], resampling_flag: bool, sampling_rate_working: int, sampling_rate_original: int|null, resampling_rate: float|null) -> tuple[np.ndarray, list[list[int]], list[list[int]]]
